channel.title: Daily arXiv Digest (JP)
channel.description: 毎朝の arXiv 注目論文10本を、NotebookLM が日本語ポッドキャストで紹介。
channel.itunes:author: M-Aji
channel.itunes:summary: 毎朝の arXiv 注目論文10本を、NotebookLM が日本語ポッドキャストで紹介。

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-13
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models — Tobias Holtdirk, Pietro Marcolongo et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13670 2. Aerial Wildfire Suppression Planning with a Hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata Fire Model — Ion Matei, Maksym Zhenirovskyy et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13633 3. EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments — Jundong Xu, Qingchuan Li et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13681 4. Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration — Zongsheng Cao, Bihao Zhan et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13669 5. Valid Inference with Synthetic Data via Task Exchangeability — Lezhi Tan, Tijana Zrnic https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.13629

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-12
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Revealing Peri-Urban Dislocation through Percolation Analysis — Dr Melissa Barrientos-Trinanes, Professor Stephen Marshall et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12399 2. Should LLM Agents Decide in Social Simulations? Comparing Finite-State and LLM-Based Decision Policies — Alejandro Buitrago López, Javier Pastor-Galindo et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12369 3. DIRECT: When and Where Should You Allocate Test-Time Compute in Embodied Planners? — Jadelynn Dao, Milan Ganai et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12402 4. Which Models Are Our Models Built On? Auditing Invisible Dependencies in Modern LLMs — Sanjay Adhikesaven, Haoxiang Sun et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12385 5. CHORUS: Decentralized Multi-Embodiment Collaboration with One VLA Policy — Ria Doshi, Tian Gao et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.12352

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-11
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Data Journalist Agent: Transforming Data into Verifiable Multimodal Stories — Kevin Qinghong Lin, Batu EI et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11176 2. FADA: Accessible fetal ultrasound interpretation and annotation with a selectively distilled unified vision-language model — Mahmood Alzubaidi, Uzair Shah et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11106 3. Flaws in the LLM Automation Narrative — George Perrett, Javae Elliott et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11166 4. COGENT: Continuous Graph Emulators with Neural Ordinary Differential Equations for Long-Term Physical Forecasting — Zesheng Liu, Maryam Rahnemoonfar https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11162 5. Monte Carlo Pass Search: Using Trajectory Generation for 3D Counterfactual Pass Evaluation in Football — Andrew Kang, Priya Narasimhan https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.11120

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-10
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Human-Centred Risk Mitigation for AI-Mediated Information Manipulation: A SOCMINT Framework Based on Information Manipulation Sets — Antonio Scala https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09754 2. Collaborative Human-Agent Protocol (CHAP) — Arsalan Shahid, Gordon Suttie et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09751 3. The Neutral Mask: How RLHF Provides Shallow Alignment while Leaving Partisan Structure Intact in a Large Language Model — Wendy K. Tam https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09735 4. Causally Evaluating the Learnability of Formal Language Tasks — Vésteinn Snæbjarnarson, Anej Svete et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09822 5. SIGA: Self-Evolving Coding-Agent Adapters for Scientific Simulation — Matthew Ho, Brian Liu et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.09774

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-09
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Agentopia: Long-Term Life Simulation and Learning in Agent Societies — Xintao Wang, Sirui Zheng et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07513 2. How AI Agents Reshape Knowledge Work: Autonomy, Efficiency, and Scope — Jeremy Yang, Kate Zyskowski et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07489 3. Modelling Opinion Dynamics at Scale with Deep MARL — Lukas Seier, Brandon Kaplowitz et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07487 4. Network Recovery from Cascade Data: A Debiased Jacobian-Based Machine Learning Approach — Lei Huang https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07483 5. Planning-aligned Token Compression for Long-Context Autonomous Driving — Zhixuan Liang, Yuxiao Chen et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.07464

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-08
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Warning Message Content Increases Help Seeking in a Large-Scale Dark Web CSAM Intervention — Caoilte Ó Ciardha, Joel Scanlan et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06417 2. Unsupervised Skill Discovery for Agentic Data Analysis — Zhisong Qiu, Kangqi Song et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06416 3. Operation-Guided Progressive Human-to-AI Text Transformation Benchmark for Multi-Granularity AI-Text Detection — Sondos Mahmoud Bsharat, Jiacheng Liu et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06481 4. Agent Memory: Characterization and System Implications of Stateful Long-Horizon Workloads — Yasmine Omri, Ziyu Gan et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06448 5. Causal Atlases from Entropic Inference: Bayesian Networks beyond Optimal DAGs — Hazhir Aliahmadi, Irina Babayan et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06440

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-06
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. RiskFlow: Fast and Faithful Safety-Critical Traffic Scenario Generation — Qi Lan, Yining Tang et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06423 2. Revising Context, Shifting Simulated Stance: Auditing LLM-Based Stance Simulation in Online Discussions — Xinnong Zhang, Wanting Shan et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06443 3. Annotation of Positive vs Negative User Interactions for Social Sign Prediction — Biancamaria Bombino, Chiara Boldrini et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06425 4. Will the Agent Recuse Itself? Measuring LLM-Agent Compliance with In-Band Access-Deny Signals — Thamilvendhan Munirathinam https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06460 5. MLEvolve: A Self-Evolving Framework for Automated Machine Learning Algorithm Discovery — Shangheng Du, Xiangchao Yan et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.06473

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-05
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Towards Efficient and Evidence-grounded Mobility Prediction with LLM-Driven Agent — Linyao Chen, Qinlao Zhao et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05130 2. Does Artificial Intelligence Advance Science? — Liangping Ding, Cornelia Lawson et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05118 3. An Open-Source Two-Stage Computer Vision Pipeline for Fine-Grained Vehicle Classification using Vision Transformers — Gandhimathi Padmanaban, Fred Feng https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05149 4. Preserving Data Privacy in Learning Causal Structure with Fully Homomorphic Encryption — Jian Yang, Yuan Tong et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05129 5. Reinforcement Learning from Rich Feedback with Distributional DAgger — Rishabh Agrawal, Jacob Fein-Ashley et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.05152

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-04
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Self-Refining Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Vision-Conditioned UAV Navigation — Roohan Ahmed Khan, Yasheerah Yaqoot et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03963 2. Language Models Need Sleep: Learning to Self-Modify and Consolidate Memories — Ali Behrouz, Farnoosh Hashemi et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03979 3. Quantifying Faithful Confidence Expression in Large Reasoning Models — Areeb Gani, Asal Meskin et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03969 4. VLESA: Vision-Language Embodied Safety Agent for Human Activity Monitoring — Hanjiang Hu, Yiyuan Pan et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03954 5. Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering for Efficient and Controllable LLM Reasoning — Yu Xia, Zhouhang Xie et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.03965

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-03
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Bridging the Last Mile of Time Series Forecasting with LLM Agents — Yuhua Liao, Zetian Wang et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02497 2. Monitoring Agentic Systems Before They're Reliable — Marisa Ferrara Boston, Glen Hanson et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02494 3. A No-Regret Framework for Adaptive Incentive Design — Georgios Vasileiou, Lantian Zhang et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02529 4. Auditing Asset-Specific Preferences in Financial Large Language Models: Evidence from Bitcoin Representations and Portfolio Allocation — Wenbin Wu https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02528 5. FigSIM: A Dataset for Fine-grained Suicide Severity and Figurative Language in Suicide Memes — Liuliu Chen, Elise R. Carrotte et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.02523

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-02
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Skill Reuse as Compression in Agentic RL — Zhikun Xu, Yu Feng et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31509 2. Evaluating Factual Density in Multi-Source RAG: A Study in Medical AI Accuracy — Michael R. DeMarco https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31506 3. Language Models Learn Constructional Semantics, Not To Mention Syntax: Investigating LM Understanding of Paired-Focus Constructions — Wesley Scivetti, Ethan Wilcox et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31586 4. What Gets Unmasked First? Trajectory Analysis of Diffusion Models for Graph-to-Text Generation — Qing Wang, Jacob Devasier et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31564 5. What Am I Missing? Question-Answering as Hidden State Probing — Chu Fei Luo, Samuel Dahan et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31561

item.title: arXiv 2026-06-01
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Stateful Online Monitoring Catches Distributed Agent Attacks — Davis Brown, Samarth Bhargav et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31593 2. LongTraceRL: Learning Long-Context Reasoning from Search Agent Trajectories with Rubric Rewards — Nianyi Lin, Jiajie Zhang et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31584 3. Disagreeing Rationales: Rethinking Classification and Explainability Evaluation in Hate Speech Detection — Benedetta Muscato, Beiduo Chen et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31563 4. Effects of Vertex Merging & Splitting on Large Coauthorship Networks: A Counterfactual Analysis — Jinseok Kim https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31555 5. Giving Sensors a Voice: Multimodal JEPA for Semantic Time-Series Embeddings — Utsav Dutta, Gerardo Pastrana et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.31580

item.title: arXiv 2026-05-31
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. Self-Trained Verification for Training- and Test-Time Self-Improvement — Chen Henry Wu, Aditi Raghunathan https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30290 2. Gram: Assessing sabotage propensities via automated alignment auditing — David Lindner, Victoria Krakovna et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30322 3. ProjectionBench: Evaluating Scientific Hypothesis Generation in LLMs Under Progressive Information Disclosure — A. J. Lew, Y. Cao et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30284 4. Reasoning with Sampling: Cutting at Decision Points — Felix Zhou, Anay Mehrotra et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30327 5. Physics Is All You Need? A Case Study in Physicist-Supervised AI Development of Scientific Software — Nhat-Minh Nguyen https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30353

item.title: arXiv 2026-05-30
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. SoundnessBench: Can Your AI Scientist Really Tell Good Research Ideas from Bad Ones? — Sy-Tuyen Ho, Minghui Liu et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30329 2. Improved Guarantees for Heterogeneous Treatment-Effect Estimation via Matrix Completion — Anay Mehrotra, Phuc Tran et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30319 3. LLMSurgeon: Diagnosing Data Mixture of Large Language Models — Yaxin Luo, Jiacheng Cui et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30348 4. Unlocking the Working Memory of Large Language Models for Latent Reasoning — Lukas Aichberger, Sepp Hochreiter https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.30343 5. item.title: arXiv 2026-05-28
item.description:
本日の注目論文：

MUSE-Autoskill: Self-Evolving Agents via Skill Creation, Memory, Management, and Evaluation — Huawei Lin, Peng Li et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27366 3. Bana et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.27371

item.title: arXiv 2026-05-27
item.description:
本日の注目論文：
1. From Model Scaling to System Scaling: Scaling the Harness in Agentic AI — Shangding Gu https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26112 2. Automated Benchmark Auditing for AI Agents and Large Language Models — Junlin Wang, Federico Bianchi et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26079 3. Claw-Anything: Benchmarking Always-On Personal Assistants with Broader Access to User's Digital World — Yusong Lin, Xinyuan Liang et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26086 4. DiscoverPhysics: Benchmarking LLMs for Out-of-the-Box Scientific Thinking — Matt L. Wiemann, Lindsay M. Smith et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26087 5. MobileGym: A Verifiable and Highly Parallel Simulation Platform for Mobile GUI Agent Research — Dingbang Wu, Rui Hao et al. https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.26114
